channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Dutch Ministry of Finance discloses breach affecting employees
item.description: The Dutch Ministry of Finance confirmed on Monday that some of its systems were breached in a cyberattack detected last week. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ghost Campaign Uses 7 npm Packages to Steal Crypto Wallets and Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a new set of malicious npm packages that are designed to steal cryptocurrency wallets and sensitive data. The activity is being tracked by ReversingLabs as the...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 5 Learnings from the First-Ever Gartner Market Guide for Guardian Agents
item.description: On February 25, 2026, Gartner published its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, marking an important milestone for this emerging category. For those unfamiliar with the various Gartner report ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: TeamPCP Hacks Checkmarx GitHub Actions Using Stolen CI Credentials
item.description: Two more GitHub Actions workflows have become the latest to be compromised by credential-stealing malware by a threat actor known as TeamPCP, the cloud-native cybercriminal operation also behind the T...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Cybersecurity Specialization: Losing Foundational Skills
item.description: Cybersecurity has changed fast. Roles are more specialized, and tooling is more advanced. On paper, this should make organizations more secure. But in practice, many teams struggle with the same basic...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: U.S. Sentences Russian Hacker to 6.75 Years for Role in $9M Ransomware Damage
item.description: A 26-year-old Russian citizen has been sentenced in the U.S. to 6.75 years (81 months) in prison for his role in assisting major cybercrime groups, including the Yanluowang ransomware crew, in conduct...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Citrix Urges Patching Critical NetScaler Flaw Allowing Unauthenticated Data Leaks
item.description: Citrix has released security updates to address two vulnerabilities in NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway, including a critical flaw that could be exploited to leak sensitive data from the applicatio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT Library to store your personal files
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out a new feature called 'Library' for ChatGPT, which allows you to store your personal files or images on OpenAI's cloud storage, so you can reference those items in a future chat. ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mazda discloses security breach exposing employee and partner data
item.description: Mazda Motor Corporation (Mazda) announced that information belonging to its employees and business partners had been exposed in a security incident detected last December. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI in the SOC: What Could Go Wrong?
item.description: Two cybersecurity leaders tested out AI in their respective SOCs for six months — and here's what they learned....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Tycoon2FA phishing platform returns after recent police disruption
item.description: The Tycoon2FA phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform that Europol and partners disrupted on March 4 has already returned to previously observed activity levels. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Targets CI/CD Secrets
item.description: A threat actor used the open source security tool to deploy an infostealer into CI/CD workflows and steal cloud credentials, SSH keys, tokens, and other sensitive secrets....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Ransomware's New Era: Moving at AI Speed
item.description: Threat actors bypass security tools and use AI to launch faster ransomware attacks that exploit valid credentials and target data...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CISOs Debate Human Role in AI-Powered Security
item.description: The idea of a "human in the loop" in AI deployment was challenged during a security executive panel at the RSAC 2026 Conference this week....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: TeamPCP deploys Iran-targeted wiper in Kubernetes attacks
item.description: The TeamPCP hacking group is targeting Kubernetes clusters with a malicious script that wipes all machines when it detects systems configured for Iran. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Crunchyroll probes breach after hacker claims to steal 6.8M users' data
item.description: Popular anime streaming platform Crunchyroll is investigating a breach after hackers claimed to have stolen personal information for approximately 6.8 million people. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: North Korean Hackers Abuse VS Code Auto-Run Tasks to Deploy StoatWaffle Malware
item.description: The North Korean threat actors behind the Contagious Interview campaign, also tracked as WaterPlum, have been attributed to a malware family tracked as StoatWaffle that's distributed via malicious Mic...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Trivy supply-chain attack spreads to Docker, GitHub repos
item.description: The TeamPCP hackers behind the Trivy supply-chain attack continued to target Aqua Security, pushing malicious Docker images and hijacking the company's GitHub organization to tamper with dozens of rep...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
item.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Attackers Hide Infostealer in Copyright Infringement Notices
item.description: A phishing campaign targeting healthcare, government, hospitality, and education sectors in various countries uses several evasion techniques to avoid detection....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Varonis Atlas: Securing AI and the Data That Powers It
item.description: AI agents can access data directly, making data security the foundation of AI security. Learn more about how Varonis Atlas helps orgs see, secure, and control AI systems and the data they can reach. [...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: CI/CD Backdoor, FBI Buys Location Data, WhatsApp Ditches Numbers & More
item.description: Another week, another reminder that the internet is still a mess. Systems people thought were secure are being broken in simple ways, showing many still ignore basic advisories. This edition covers a ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Online service change causes email access issues
item.description: Microsoft is working to address an ongoing service issue that has intermittently prevented some users from accessing their cloud-based Exchange Online mailboxes via Outlook mobile and Mac desktop clie...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: We Found Eight Attack Vectors Inside AWS Bedrock. Here's What Attackers Can Do with Them
item.description: AWS Bedrock is Amazon's platform for building AI-powered applications. It gives developers access to foundation models and the tools to connect those models directly to enterprise data and systems. Th...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Warns IRS Phishing Hits 29,000 Users, Deploys RMM Malware
item.description: Microsoft has warned of fresh campaigns that are capitalizing on the upcoming tax season in the U.S. to harvest credentials and deliver malware. The email campaigns take advantage of the urgency and t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: FBI warns of Handala hackers using Telegram in malware attacks
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned network defenders that Iranian hackers linked to the country's Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) are using Telegram in malware attacks....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA orders feds to patch DarkSword iOS flaws exploited attacks
item.description: CISA ordered U.S. government agencies to patch three iOS vulnerabilities targeted in cryptocurrency theft and cyberespionage attacks using the DarkSword exploit kit. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Trivy Hack Spreads Infostealer via Docker, Triggers Worm and Kubernetes Wiper
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered malicious artifacts distributed via Docker Hub following the Trivy supply chain attack, highlighting the widening blast radius across developer environments. T...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New KB5085516 emergency update fixes Microsoft account sign-in
item.description: Microsoft has released an emergency update to address a major issue that breaks sign-ins with Microsoft accounts across multiple Microsoft apps, including Teams and OneDrive. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-32975 (CVSS 10.0) to Hijack Unpatched Quest KACE SMA Systems
item.description: Threat actors are suspected to be exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance (SMA), according to Arctic Wolf. The cybersecurity company said it obser...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
